question_title: Will the interest in “joint comprehensive plan of action” change between 2026-04-15 and 2026-04-24 according to Google Trends?
summary: The search term "joint comprehensive plan of action" spiked to 100 on Apr 12 due to US-Iran Islamabad talks and Democrats specifically citing the JCPOA. By Apr 15, it had crashed to 6. The baseline for this niche policy term is 0-1. The key tension is between natural post-spike decay to baseline (favoring Decreases) and the Apr 21 ceasefire deadline (potentially triggering another spike, favoring Increases). The "Doesn't change" band (3-9) is narrow and the term behaves bimodally. Decreases is most likely because the default trajectory is return to 0-1 and the term is too niche for general Iran coverage to sustain it. However, the ceasefire deadline, possible war resumption, and continued Democratic political messaging citing JCPOA give meaningful probability to Increases.

factors.description: Natural baseline for this term is 0-1; 90% of non-spike days show 0-1. Post-spike decay from 100→6 in 2 days shows rapid reversion. Without a catalyst, Apr 24 value will be 0-1 (Decreases).

US-Iran ceasefire expires Apr 21, 3 days before resolution. Failed Islamabad talks, mediators trying to restart. War could resume. Near-certain catalyst for Iran-related news coverage that may reference JCPOA.

Democrats actively using JCPOA as political weapon. House Foreign Affairs Committee cited JCPOA by name on Apr 12, driving the spike. Continued political debate likely generates JCPOA-specific searches around ceasefire deadline.

The term 'joint comprehensive plan of action' is very niche. Most Iran-related searches use 'Iran deal', 'Iran war', 'ceasefire'. The Apr 12 spike was unusually driven by specific political messaging that may not recur.
Extremely fast decay pattern (100→6 in 2 days). Even if a new spike occurs around Apr 21, by Apr 24 it may already be back to low single digits, making 'Increases' harder to achieve.
SerpAPI vs pytrends measurement uncertainty of ±2-3 points. At low absolute values (0-9), this can flip outcomes across the ±3 resolution threshold.
If war resumes after ceasefire expiry, coverage would be sustained rather than spike-and-decay, keeping JCPOA elevated through Apr 24.
factors.conditional: If ceasefire breaks down and war resumes

reasoning:
**Forecast: Will "joint comprehensive plan of action" interest change between Apr 15-24?**

| Outcome | Probability |
|---------|------------|
| **Decreases** | **40%** |
| **Increases** | **30%** |
| **Doesn't change** | **30%** |

**Key reasoning:**

- **Starting value is 6** (post-spike from 100 on Apr 12, already collapsed). Baseline for this niche term is 0-1.
- **Threshold arithmetic**: Decreases needs Apr 24 ≤ 2, Doesn't change needs 3-9, Increases needs ≥ 10.
- **Decreases is favored** because the natural trajectory is decay to the 0-1 baseline (90% of non-spike days), and this very specific policy term doesn't capture general Iran news searches.
- **Increases is meaningful** because the Apr 21 ceasefire deadline is a major catalyst — if war resumes or new talks reference JCPOA, sustained coverage could push Apr 24 above 10. Democrats have been specifically citing "JCPOA" as a political weapon.
- **Doesn't change is the narrowest band** (3-9) and this term behaves bimodally (either baseline or spiking), making a moderate middle value less likely. SerpAPI measurement uncertainty adds some probability here.
sources_consulted: [(Google Trends) joint comprehensive plan of action](https://trends.google.com/trends/explore?q=joint comprehensive plan of action)
[(Metaculus) Will the interest in “joint comprehensive plan of action” change between 2026-04-15 and 2026-04-24 according to Google Trends?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/43215)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves “Doesn't change” if the value on the timeline at [https://trends.google.com/trends/explore?geo=US&tz=0&q=joint%20comprehensive%20plan%20of%20action&date=2026-03-25%202026-04-24](https://trends.google.com/trends/explore?geo=US&tz=0&q=joint%20comprehensive%20plan%20of%20action&date=2026-03-25%202026-04-24) for 2026-04-24 is within 3 of the value at 2026-04-15. It resolves “Increases” if the value is more than 3 greater, and resolves “Decreases” if the value is more than 3 lower.
fine_print:
A script will be used to determine the resolution of this question. It will access the data on Google Trends using [SerpApi](https://serpapi.com/), and compare the height of the timeline for the two aforementioned dates. The specific python query will be structured as follows:
```
from serpapi import GoogleSearch

search = GoogleSearch(params={
  "api_key": API_KEY,
  "engine": "google_trends",
  "data_type": "TIMESERIES",
  "geo": "US",
  "tz": 0,
  "q": "joint comprehensive plan of action",
  "date": "2026-03-25 2026-04-24",
})
search.get_dict()
```
Note that there may be differences between the results returned by the API and the data appearing on the page. This seems to be due to the 'tz' parameter not having the intended effect in-browser. In this case, the API results will be considered authoritative.
Note that the precision of the timeline will be 1 day, so this will compare the overall interest for the whole day as determined by Google Trends.
Dates are determined in UTC.
If the data is no longer available, or the script fails, this question will be annulled or manually resolved by a moderator.
